rulings.Case Title: DANIEL BOREEN VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Dismissal For Failure To Prosecute
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 11, 2024, Line 1. RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion For Dismissal For Failure To Prosecute. Defendant's unopposed "motion for dismissal for failure to prosecute" is granted. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: DOROTHY RIEDEL ET AL VS. JOHN MUNGUIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION For Assignment Order [And For Order Restraining Judgment Debtor]; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 11, 2024, Line 17. CROSS COMPLAINANT JOHN MUNGUIA's MOTION For Assignment Order [And For Order Restraining Judgment Debtor]. Defendant John Munguia's motion for assignment order is granted in part and denied in part. Pursuant to CCP 708.510, the court orders assignment of judgment debtor Dorothy Riedel's right to payment from the following assets to judgment creditor John Nunguia to service the outstanding amended judgment: 1. AAA Guaranteed Issue Whole Life Insurance Policy Certificate No. 4032943641 - $10,000; 2. AAA Flexible Premium Adjustable Life Insurance Policy Certificate No. 5300022623 - $100,000; 3. AAA Guaranteed Issue Whole Life Insurance Policy Certificate No. 4032957096 - $15,000; 4. Prudential Insurance Company - $100,000; 5. Prudential Insurance Company - $10,000; 6. All rights in Trad'r Sam, a California Partnership; and 7. The type 21 and 48 liquors licenses identified as Alcohol Beverage Control License Number 550511. 8. Wells Fargo "Stocks and Investment Accounts" Policy No. xxx1204; Creditor Munguia sufficiently demonstrates that debtor Riedel has an interest in the above-listed assets and assignment is appropriate. (McCann Decl., Exs. 5-8, 12.) The motion is denied as to the remaining items because creditor Munguia failed to obtain his evidentiary burden. For example, an attorney declaration based on information and belief fails to show an ownership interest in Helodedo, Inc. (McCann Decl., par. 15.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: BRYANT FU VS. SHUZHEN TU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens Or Alt, To Require Claimant To Provide Undertaking
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 11, 2024, Line 19. 1 - DEFENDANT VI DAN TRAN's Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens Or Alt, To Require Claimant To Provide Undertaking. Defendant Vi Dan Tran's motion to expunge lis pendens is denied. This court denied a nearly identical motion by Tran on September 11, 2023. CCP 1008 indicates that a party moving for reconsideration of a prior order "shall state by affidavit what application was made before, when and to what judge, what order or decisions were made, and what new or different facts, circumstances, or law are claimed to be shown." (CCP 1008(a) and (b).) Tran has failed to mention the prior order. The motion is denied for failure to comply with the code. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: JAY ZALOWITZ VS. DAVID SMOOKE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp Sections 128.5 And 128.7 Re Plaintiff'S Motion To Disqualify
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 11, 2024, Line 8. DEFENDANT DAVID SMOOKE AN INDIVIDUAL, LINH SMOOKE, AND ARTMAP, INC. D/B/A/ HACKERNOON's Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp Sections 128.5 And 128.7 Re Plaintiff'S Motion To Disqualify. Defendants' "motion for sanctions pursuant to CCP sections 128.5 and 128.7 re plaintiff's motion to disqualify" is denied. While unsuccessful, the disqualification motion was not in bad faith, frivolous, solely intended to cause delay, harassing or without evidentiary support. The court notes a pattern of meritless sanctions motions by both sides in this litigation. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: GRANT M. SAXENA VS. PATRICK S. MCCORMICK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Disqualify Counsel
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 11, 2024, Line 10. PLAINTIFF GRANT SAXENA's Motion For Order To Disqualify Counsel. Pro per plaintiff's largely incomprehensible motion to disqualify counsel is denied, without prejudice to filing an understandable motion. The current motion's lead argument is that two defendants "are not entitled to any representation in this civil action," but why is not articulated. (Mot. 3:11-12.) Nor are the motion's unclear allegations as to "privileged and confidential information" supported by any competent evidence. (Id. at 5:1-15.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)
